verdict: Anisotropic 3-D coding (vertically elongated fields + impaired vertical odometry, Hayman 2011) EMERGES from gravity-biased experience, never hardcoded. With isotropic hardware (isotropic code noise, isotropic init, one shared power budget) a capacity-limited code allocates resolution to well-experienced axes by rate-distortion/water-filling; the low-experience vertical axis falls below the noise floor and is disproportionately under-coded (normalized vertical/horizontal error ratio ~3x). FALSIFIER: isotropic experience gives an isotropic code (ratio ~1, the flying-bat/Ginosar regime), so the anisotropy is experience, not architecture; a dose-response confirms it tracks the vertical deficit. Honest note: absolute vertical error is small (small range), so only the normalized resolution measure reveals the disproportionate loss.
